summary: postinstall exfiltrates /app source, environment variables, and HTB flags to hardcoded remote IP
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js gates execution on `INIT_CWD === "/app"` and skips entirely when the configured registry matches Chinese mirror or scanner names (npmmirror, tencent, cnpm, taobao, huawei, ustc, hscan, nijin). When the gate passes, the script shells out via `child_process` to dump `/app` source files (main.rs, Cargo.toml, package.json), git history, any files containing the `HTB{` flag substring, and the full output of `env | sort`. The collected data is chunked and PUT over plain HTTP to `http://127.0.0.1:30782` and `http://154.57.164.71:30782` (hardcoded in postinstall.js line 5 as `TARGETS`). The combination of narrow installer-environment targeting, anti-scanner registry evasion, and exfiltration of environment variables plus flag-bearing files to a hardcoded remote IP is unambiguous attacker tradecraft against installers running this package in the targeted environment.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
